issue_title: `maximum()`: out-of-place returns incorrect unsigned output on type promotion.
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `actionable` + `high priority`. Dual maintainer signal confirms issue is both acknowledged and prioritized.

linked_prs.title: [CUDA] Fix torch.maximum/minimum incorrect results with mixed dtypes
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Still OPEN and actively worked. Has 3 COLLABORATOR approvals (Aidyn-A approved twice, eqy approved once). Blocked by unintended behavioral changes in a recent commit that COLLABORATOR Aidyn-A flagged on 2026-03-12. Contributor needs to clean up the PR to remove the unintended changes before it can be merged.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR Aidyn-A: APPROVED 2026-01-26, CHANGES_REQUESTED 2026-01-30 (extra files), APPROVED 2026-02-27. COLLABORATOR eqy: APPROVED 2026-01-29. COLLABORATOR nikitaved: COMMENTED with technical feedback. Last blocker (2026-03-12): Aidyn-A flagged unintended behavioral changes in the latest commit and said 'it is not ready'. No pytorchbot merge request issued.

linked_prs.title: [CUDA] Fix maximum/minimum incorrect output on mixed-dtype out= promotion
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Alternative fix PR, still OPEN with no reviews. May be a parallel attempt by a different contributor.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: No reviews from any maintainer. Very early stage.

linked_prs.title: Fix maximum/minimum CUDA type promotion with out= argument
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Explicitly closed by the author as a duplicate of PR #173170: 'Closing as duplicate of #173170 which has the same fix and is further along in review.'
linked_prs.reviews_summary: No reviews before closure. Closed as duplicate.

maintainer_signals.author: Aidyn-A
maintainer_signals.quote: @achintya-p, please fix the lint. The rest looks good!

maintainer_signals.author: Aidyn-A
maintainer_signals.quote: As much as I want this PR to be merged, it is not ready. This PR includes some behavioral changes that we do not want to have.

confidence_justification: Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: COLLABORATOR approved_fix signals from Aidyn-A and eqy on PR #173170 → +0.20. Running total: 0.95. Step 3: Repro script present — minimal 6-line CUDA code block with exact wrong output shown (247 vs expected 1) and user-provided root cause hypothesis → +0.15. Running total: 1.10. Step 4: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.20. Step 5: fix_scope is 'single_function' — CUDA kernel dispatch fix changing iter.dtype() to iter.common_dtype() in the maximum/minimum kernel, confirmed by PR #178211 body → +0.10. Running total: 1.30. Step 6: Clamp to 1.0 before negatives. Step 7: Aidyn-A partial_fix comment ('includes behavioral changes we do not want') — not a full rejection, more a cleanup requirement; PR still has 3 COLLABORATOR approvals. Apply -0.15 for the open PR not being final-merge-ready. Running total: 0.85. No hard blockers. Final: 0.85.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to positive_71774: silent correctness bug on CUDA with mixed dtypes, correctness+actionable labels, multiple COLLABORATOR approvals. Key differences: (1) the fix PR #173170 is still OPEN (not closed) making it easier to pick up; (2) multiple COLLABORATORs are actively engaged; (3) the remaining blocker is a cleanup issue (removing unintended behavioral changes) rather than an ambiguous merge status. This is arguably more actionable than positive_71774 because the PR is still open and the required fix is clear.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #173170 ('[CUDA] Fix torch.maximum/minimum incorrect results with mixed dtypes') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.
contribution_path.warning: What killed this PR: Still OPEN and actively worked. Has 3 COLLABORATOR approvals (Aidyn-A approved twice, eqy approved once). Blocked by unintended behavioral changes in a recent commit that COLLABORATOR Aidyn-A flagged

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #173110. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: PR #173170 has 3 COLLABORATOR approvals (Aidyn-A × 2, eqy) and is almost ready. The only remaining blocker is lint failures and extra unrelated files included in the diff. Strip the extra files, run `lintrunner` locally, and rebase onto main — the fix itself is approved.
contribution_path.warning: Aidyn-A issued CHANGES_REQUESTED specifically for 'extra files' in the diff. Keep the diff minimal: only change files directly related to the maximum() unsigned fix.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_torch.py -k maximum`. Also run `lintrunner` locally — the CHANGES_REQUESTED from Aidyn-A was triggered by lint failures, not logic issues.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #173110' and 'Revives #173170 and #177373' in the body. Tag @Aidyn-A @eqy for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
contribution_path.warning: PR #173170 is still open with 3 approvals — the right move may be to push fixes to that branch rather than opening a new PR.
